title: Inspiration trap creativity workshop
description:
Live African music and drumming events in NJ
6,7&8pm Issyra Gallery presents - Inspiration Trap Creativity workshop

Only 10 attendees allowed social distancing and mandatory mask wearing.

You are invited to participate in a special session of creative thinking and fresh insight dedicated to inspiration.

We live in a world of constant change that requires us to be able to continually adapt and innovate in order to succeed. Unfortunately, many of us have forgotten how to delve into our raw creativity and leave behind the rigidity of the frameworks we have progressively interiorized.
This unique workshop seeks to reclaim our inspiration and imagination, rethink the unthinkable. It is ideal for people working in the creative arts as well as students, entrepreneurs, team managers, corporate leaders and anyone looking to implement their creative vision.

Inspiration is a vital driving force in our lives that moves us to create our best work and lights the way for new, authentic ideas. How do we invite, cultivate and harness inspiration so that we are able to bring these ideas into reality?
As a self-taught artist exploring various avenues of self-expression, Issa Sow draws a distinction between the phenomenon of inspiration itself and our task of making ourselves ready for it.
In the upcoming Inspiration Trap workshop, we will imagine ways we can invite and contain our inspiration, and well as keep our mind in the open, dynamic state as a receptacle or a “canvas” to collect, reflect and grow our ideas.
Feel free to bring along a personal object, a piece of clothing or even a small piece of furniture to play with during this hands-on experiment in innovative thinking. There are surprises and new elements in each session!

Issa =2
Issa Sow is a contemporary international autodidact artist known as Issa=2.
He believes in life being a journey of continual growth with each new

description: Rick Lopez, author of the forthcoming Sam Rivers Sessionography, will be discussing the legacy of this jazz great. This event will be held on the first floor, Plymouth Street side.

title: Raised Bed Gardening with Bracco Farms
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
Ready to take your gardening game to the next level? Join us for an engaging and informative talk all about the power of the Raised Bed Garden!

This talk will be hosted by Bracco Farms. They will discuss the benefits of raised garden beds include better soil drainage, easy access to plants, portability, soil customization increased soil control, and also the ability to keep weeds and pests at bay. Bracco Farms will discuss everything from planning to growing in a Raised Bed Garden. We will demonstrate how almost every kind of plant will grow better in it for many reasons which we will point out. They will also bring, show and demonstrate many of the hand tools used in a Raised Bed garden.

Please note this event will take place in the Main Library's Auditorium.

title: Cru Large Group!
description:
Cru is a caring community passionate about connecting people to Jesus Christ! Join us in learning together on Who Christ is, building a personal relationship with Him, and joining a community filled with love and open arms! 
This semester, we will be focusing on the theme "Identity in Christ." We pray that our students will learn more about who they are in Christ, and look at some examples of people in the Bible where God revealed their true identity in Christ!
"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. description:
Viola Masterclass with Melissa Reardon
Free and Open to the Public
Grammy-nominated violist Melissa Reardon of the Borromeo String Quartet will lead a masterclass in viola performance and chamber music collaboration, and expressive string interpretation.
Bio
Grammy-nominated violist Melissa Reardon is an internationally renowned performer whose solo and chamber playing spans all musical genres. Melissa is the Artistic Director of the Portland Chamber Music Festival in Portland, ME, violist of the Borromeo String Quartet, Artist-in-Residence at Bard College and Conservatory, and a founding member and Executive Director of the East Coast Chamber Orchestra (ECCO).

title: The Secret Tea Room at Van Vleck - 2PM JUST ADDED!
description:
Seating times: 
April 10 
11:30 AM – 1:00 PM  SOLD OUT
12:00 PM – 1:30 PM SOLD OUT
1:00 PM – 2:30 PM  SOLD OUT
New session just added!!! 2:00 PM
2:30 PM – 4:00 PM  SOLD OUT
Join us for an enchanting Cream Tea in the Living Room at Van Vleck House! 
Upon arrival, you will be offered a menu of fine teas and infusions. Your choice of tea will be brewed to perfection. Once everyone is settled in, the tiers will arrive with freshly baked scones, jam, homemade clotted cream, scrumptious sandwiches, and a dessert. We hope you will join us! 
This event is for adults and children ages 13+. For group bookings of more than 8 guests, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. Seatings are 90 minutes, and registration is required. 
Dietary Restrictions
A gluten-free option is available with prior notice. We are not able to accommodate a dairy-free or vegan diet. All requests must be communicated to us 48 hours before the event. You may note this request on the checkout page. For dietary/allergy inquiries, please contact richard@secrettearoom.us. 
Cancellation Policy
We are unable to accommodate cancellations or rescheduling. If you find you are no longer able to attend your scheduled event, you may transfer your ticket(s) to another person(s) to attend instead. Please notify Van Vleck House and Gardens so we can update your ticket name(s) for event check-in. 
Please note that this event is a fundraiser, and a portion of your ticket price will go towards supporting our beloved Van Vleck House & Gardens. 
Member Price: $45
General Price: $55
A 10% gratuity for your server is included in tea pricing.

All employees must be current students, registered for Fall 2026. Incoming students can be hired starting July 1st. We offer part-time and full-time hours at both indoor and outdoor facilities. We will work around class schedules, but all staff must be able to work a minimum of 14 hours (part-time), and 32 hours (full-time). Full time staff get free housing at the Village.
Currently certified lifeguards - we accept all major certifications, and can recertify you for free. 
Non-certified students - Students that are not currently certified can take a pretest for free, before you register for a lifeguard training course. If you pass the pretest, and can work a minimum of 14 hours per week for the summer (average), than we will certify you for free. If you can work full-time, we may also be able to offer free housing for the summer (space is limited, so this option is first-come, first-serve).
Swim tests will take place between March 23rd and April 10th, 2026.

title: Outdoor Adventures - Arbor Day Everyday
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Saturday, 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
April 11th – Arbor Day Everyday 
Arbor Day is a day to reflect on the importance of trees in our environment. We have an abundance of beautiful trees throughout the gardens. At April’s Outdoor Adventures, learn more about our trees as we explore their role in our environment, their unique leaf shapes, and what animals use trees as their homes.  
Join us in the gardens as you and your family explore and learn about the outdoor world through crafts, scavenger hunts, activity sheets and educational displays. Each program will focus on a specific theme, and our Van Vleck Education team will guide your family through this interactive learning experience. This program is entirely outdoors, weather dependent. Registration is required and includes everything you need to participate in the program. Designed for children ages 4 -11 but all are welcome. 
Fee: FREE for Members of Van Vleck; $15 per family  for  General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class 
 
Sponsored by  
  
Upcoming Outdoor Adventures: 
May 16th – Honeybees & Hummingbirds 
What do bees and hummingbirds have in common? They fly and they are pollinators! Join us to learn about your local pollinators, how they make a positive impact on our environment, and what we can do to help them on their mission.  
  
June 6th – Insect Safari  
You can find many different insects buzzing, wiggling, and crawling around the gardens. Join us for an Insect Safari at this month’s Outdoor Adventures. Create a bug inspired craft, take a hike looking for insects, and learn about the important roles insects play in the garden.

title: Daytime Pool at Clifton Billiards
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
***Trying something different this time, Saturday afternoon date and in the window for day passes! So if you are available and feel like staying longer, or coming closer to the middle or end, it's more flexible, easier to change between tables and play with more people and less time is spent counting hours, etc. We'll see how this pans out going forward.***

***Meeting Point:***
Outside of the building; After 5-10 minutes: You can meet us upstairs at the pool tables. *\*\*Please look for the group as the staff do not keep up with the group\*\***
*** Price:
Photo of the day passes pricing is posted, however if you only want to stay a short while and would rather pay less, the price is $6.50 per hour per person if you have 4 players at your table. However, the final price is based on the total amount of minutes played. At the end of the hour, the table amount will be split per person. Each table paying this way should have a table leader, **this person will collect the money of players at their table and will pay for the table on one card.** 3-4 table leaders will be needed, **volunteering for this is encouraged**

***Bringing cash is highly encouraged to pay your portion to your table leader***

*Parking*: street parking is available however it may be hard to find close to the venue. There are side streets before the town proper, but a good option is the nearby ANT bookstore which has a lot which should be okay to use.

***This location has food & drinks available for purchase.***

*** Expectations**:
Pool cues are available at the location, expect mid to low lighting. If you come after 7:15pm, look for the Meetup group upstairs.
** *What if I'm a beginner or only want to play with people at a similar level?**
Tables will be divided between beginners, intermediate players, and advanced. However, this depends on how many types of players come, so it will be divided as best as possibly available. If you would like game rules, please speak with Marc.** Marc is also available to answer questions including how to hold the cue, stances, etc.**
*** Is anyone at Clifton Billiards after the event is over?
Afterward, most go home or go to a local bar. If you'd like to hang out after, definitely speak with members to see if they want to do something after.
*** Can I switch tables?**
*Yes, but it does make it easier if you stay at your table. If you switch tables, pay the amount of time you were at that table. Example, if you were at able A for an hour, pay the hour to the table A leader. If you go table B for two hours, pay your two hours to the table B leader. At the end, the table leader is taking the payments for the total amount of time at that table.*
*** How do I get balls for the table and how many people do I tell staff that I have?*
*One person per table gives their ID to the staff in order to check out balls. Again, the price for 4 people is about $6.50. If you have more or less, it slightly changes. So it just depends on how many people are at your table. It may be better to mention that you have more people esp if you are starting with 4 or 5 people.*
*** I feel like people aren't trying to be my friend.*
*Please remember that different people have different goals. Some may come to just play billiards, others may be there to meet new people or do something social, and some may be there to make long term friends. Find people that match your goals or be ok with others that may not.
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: NJ Code & Coffee meetup - April
description:
NJ Code & Coffee
Calling all New Jersey techies to the **NJ Code and Coffee** tech community meetup at the EqualSpace ES550 Tech & Innovation campus!

Code and Coffee is an inclusive, informal networking/co-working session for those in tech (and the tech-curious).
That includes operators, founders, and investors. Developers, data engineers, UX/UI designers, data scientists, product managers, software engineers, solution architects... **all are welcome!**

People of all skill and experience levels can attend, we love it that way. Many people (optionally) bring projects to work on, and many other people (optionally) socialize the entire time. It's entirely up to you!

Come grab a cup of coffee and meet new people, share stories, find collaborators, talk about everything tech, talk about everything NOT tech, take a break from job applications, get away from the computer screen, make connections, make conversation, **make friends!**

**Agenda:**
**2pm:** Doors open, networking starts
**2:30pm:** Intro circle
**3pm:** networking continues
**5pm:** event ends

**The Intro Circle**
**Near the beginning of the event (about 2:30 pm), we will do a standup in a circle:**
We first talk about organizer announcements, updates, and logistics. Then we go around the circle where each person says:

* your name and job title/ role in tech/ tech stack,
* What you can help others with,
* What you would like help with,
* Job opportunities you're hiring for OR announce that you are looking for one. If none, that's cool.

After the introduction circle, everything is self-organized.

**Community Discord - come hang out before and after!:**
[www.codeandcoffee.chat](www.codeandcoffee.chat)

**The Venue**
Equal Space is a premium share-space created with multicultural business owners in mind, =SPACE equips founders with resources, programming and a powerful network.

title: SMC Trail Work Volunteer Day- CHANGED DATE
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
**TRAIL WORK** will begin this year with finishing work on the Lenape Trail reroute below the Beech Brook Cascades. The steps are in (see photo), but final tread improvements on switchbacks and throughout are needed. We will meet at the Dog Park (11 Bear Lane) and head down to the Lenape trail from there.

Trail work is physically challenging but very satisfying as we use hand tools to build and repair trails. Participants must be 15+ years. Those younger than 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Participants should wear long pants due to [ticks](https://www.somocon.org/?page_id=193928&fb) and sturdy shoes, and bring gloves (though backup gloves will be available). Tools, water, and a snack will be provided. (Registration is not required though if you sign up on Meetup, it helps us know how many tools to bring.)

title: Croton Aqueduct Bike Trip
description:
Get ready for a scenic spin through history as we ride the legendary Old Croton Aqueduct! This unforgettable bike trip takes you along one of New York’s most beautiful and peaceful trails, where tree-lined paths, and charming river views, create the perfect backdrop for a day outdoors. As we cruise along the trail you’ll enjoy plenty of photo-worthy stops along the way. Expect a relaxed pace with time to explore, snack, and soak in the scenery. Whether you're in it for the fresh air, the history, or just a joyful ride with great company, this Croton Aqueduct adventure is a perfect blend of nature, culture, and fun on two wheels! 
How do I sign-up?

Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students - $10 | Faculty/Staff - $20

When and Where?

Sunday, April 12, 2026
10:00 am - 5:00 pm
Croton Aqueduct, NJ - Begins and ends at Student Recreation Center

What to bring:

Money for spending, lunch, water, comfortable shoes, sunscreen.

Potential Hazards: Hazardous weather, poisonous plants/allergies, insect bites, unpredictable weather changes, sunburn/heat exhaustion, dehydration, and injuries, including sprains/strains, abrasions, lacerations and fractures.
 

Hosted by: Campus Recreation

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12267448

description:
Green Team Ministry’s December Meeting will meet VIRTUALLY Wednesday, Dec. 10 at 7pm.
(Usually meets 2nd Sundays) 12-1pm in the FCC Library
Be part of planning events, celebrating Creation,  and living your faith by taking action for the planet. Email greenteam@fccmtc.com for Zoom link.
 
 
Green Team/Creation Justice Ministry monthly meetings: 2nd Sunday of the month, after worship, in the FCC library.  Our congregation is committed to the idea that creation care is central to each aspect of our church: our theology and worship, institutional life and practices, and our partnership and work in the wider community. Learn about FCC’s climate justice initiatives, be part of planning our events, and live your faith by taking action for the planet. 
Check out our Green Team Ministry page here: https://fccmontclair.org/green-team-ministry/
and read our Creation Justice Covenant here: https://www.fccmontclair.org/creation-justice-church/

description: A weekly introduction to poetry covering haiku, sonnet, blank & free verse, are covered in depth. Kids will learn historical significance, structure, and grammatical conventions. Kids will also make art based on their poems, further demonstrating their creativity. Facilitated by poet Jack Jerow. Registration recommended for reminders.

title: Second Sunday Concert: Teatro Nuovo
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
April 12, 2PM 
Led by conductor/musicologist Will Crutchfield, Teatro Nuovo – Italian for “new theater” – is a young organization devoted to cutting-edge interpretation of classic Italian opera.  Since its debut in 2018, Teatro Nuovo has presented thirteen operas by Rossini, Donizetti, Bellini, Verdi, Ricci, and Mayr, culminating in the modern premiere of Carolina Uccelli’s Anna di Resburgo, which made the New York Times list of the 20 best Classical events for 2024.  This year Teatro Nuovo will celebrate the bicentennial of Italian opera in America.

This concert will show how delving into forgotten traditions can animate the future of the art, as Will Crutchfield and young vocal stars will perform virtuoso thrillers and heart-melting lyrics from the golden age of Italian Bel Canto. Don’t miss this exciting opportunity to hear some of opera’s newest rising stars! 
Members received the registration link in mid-March. This concert is free but a donation of $10 is appreciated to continue to fund the series. Registrations are recommended but there are always a few seats available just before the start of the concert. 

 
Many thanks to our Series Patrons.

title: Mah Jongg: Spring Open Play
location: Montclair Main - Cafe Area, Montclair Public Library
description:
PLEASE NOTE: You must sign up for EACH INDIVIDUAL SESSION. Signing up for one session does not register you for any following classes.

The Mah Jongg Meetup provides a weekly opportunity for players to continue practicing their established skills, sharpen their strategies, build confidence at the game table, and socialize with fellow Mah Jongg players!

The library will provide five reserved tables in the cafe! 

NOTE: Attendees should bring their personal sets to this meetup and should already have an understanding of the Mah Jongg game. Attendees should bring their 2025 or 2026 National Mah Jongg League card to the meetup. Bringing an additional set is encouraged!

*Note: There will be a limited number of Mah Jongg sets available on a first-come, first-served basis.

If you're new to the game, or don't feel comfortable sitting at a table and playing with others at a similar level, please register for a Beginner class by clicking here.

 

IMPORTANT REMINDERS:


	Students should have taken Beginner Mah Jongg or have equivalent knowledge and experience. 
	Students should bring the 2025 National Mah Jongg League card to Open Play.

title: Memory Café - A Dementia-Friendly Social Gathering
description:
For adults with dementia and their caregivers, this event offers a welcoming, safe, calming space for socializing, music, art and reminiscing every month.


A “Memory Café” is a dementia-friendly social gathering for individuals with early-stage memory loss or mild cognitive decline and their care givers.

The meetings will be held in a safe and comfortable environment where people can interact and socialize with others having similar experiences. At the gatherings participants can get support in a non-judgmental setting, while enjoying the benefits of music for the brain.

The meetings will be run by Lisy Espindola, LCSW and Kim McLennon, MSW and music therapist. Lunch and/or snacks will be served.

This is a joint program between Lifelong Montclair at the Township of Montclair and the Montclair Public Library.

Attendance is free but registration is required. Questions? Call 973-744-3278.

To stay informed, sign up for one or all of our eNewsletters, click here.

Photographs may be taken during our programs and events for promotional purposes such as social media and e-newsletters. If you do not wish to have your photograph taken please let us know before the event.

title: Community and Social Services Career Expo
description:
Community and Social Services Career ExpoDate: April 13, 2026Time: 3:30pm-6:30pmLocation: University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)
Registration is available on Handshake: https://app.joinhandshake.com/career_fairs/37dfb726-9582-4e23-9c82-6561adbc3e11/student_preview
 
Description:
Calling all community and service-minded students exploring clinical and non-clinical career options!
 
Are you looking for a job or internship? Are you curious about the career paths you can pursue with your major or graduate program? If so, the Community and Social Services Career Expo is an amazing opportunity to meet employers that align with your professional interests. Whether you are a freshman or a graduate student, this event is designed to help you explore career possibilities, gain networking experience by talking with employers, and make connections that may eventually lead to a job or internship. There will be fun mixed in with the opportunity to win raffle prizes! 
 
More Information:- Mark your calendar! Monday, April 13, 3:30pm - 6:30pm, University Hall Conference Center (7th Floor)- Visit with non-profit, government, and corporate employers that have a service-oriented mission- Discover opportunities with campus organizations and departments, including international experiences that incorporate social impact- Pre-registration is strongly encouraged - Register on Handshake!- Event details and employer list can be found on Handshake. - Dress neatly and bring resumes! Check out Rocky's Closet for free professional attire. 
 
This event is ideal for graduate and undergraduate students seeking jobs and internships in: Counseling, Child Advocacy, Justice Studies, Social Work, Political Science, Psychology and so much more!
 
Co-sponsored by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences, College for Community Health, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, Office of Experiential Education and Career Connections, Office of Government Relations, Office of International Academic Initiatives
 
Questions? Contact Jennifer Dudeck-Lenis at dudecklenisj@montclair.edu.
 

Hosted by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS), College for Community Health, Government Relations and Community Affairs , Office of International Academic Initiatives, Office of Community Engagement and Partnerships, The Office for Experiential Education and Career Connections

title: Practical Spanish 1
location: Montclair Main - Classroom, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

This class is for those of you who know little to no Spanish and are interested in acquiring some practical communication skills. You will be introduced to basic greetings and practice useful questions and answers. You will learn the fundamentals of Spanish pronunciation while mastering introductory grammar and vocabulary.
 
Topics covered:


	conversation/questions
	greetings
	personal information
	expressions of courtesy
	feelings
	asking for direction
	talk about needs and wants
	where people are from
	Alphabet
	Numbers/days/date
	Colors
	Basic commands
	Some ar/er/ir  regular verbs
	Verb to be: ser/estar
	Verb to go: ir
	Verbs: tener/necesitar/querer
	Ir   a
	Negation
	Subject pronouns/difference between tú/usted
	Gender and number of nouns/definite/indefinite articles
	Some adjectives including cuánto/a
	The expression “Hay”
	Basic geography, culture and food

title: Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair
description:
**Midday MeetUp - Jews and Baseball, A Love Affair**  
  
A monthly gathering to make connections and learn together.  
11:45am Catered Lunch (free)  
12:30pm Featured Program   
  
Join baseball historian Sam Bernstein for a lively survey of Jewish involvement in America’s National Pastime.  
  
Cost:** FREE or Suggested Donation of $10 per person to offset costs. (Bill My Account is only open to Shomrei Members)  
  
**RSVP:** Required so we know how much food to buy.  
https://shomrei.easyshul.com/events/6967e303368f110026c0c410  
  
**\***Members & PreSchool Families should use their fob to access the building.  
  
**About the Program**  
Using audio and video, *Jews and Baseball* explores the origins of the game and the impact Jewish players, owners, and innovators have had on its evolution. The program highlights figures such as Hank Greenberg, Sandy Koufax, Barney Dreyfuss, Bud Selig, Lou Boudreau, Marvin Miller, as well as the game's current Jewish stars. It also delves into topics including antisemitism in the sport, baseball’s growth in Israel, and the lore of the “Koufax Curse.”  
  
**About Sam Bernstein**  
Sam Bernstein is a retired Social Worker who has published many articles on baseball history. He received a BA in History from the American University and an MSW from Adelphi University. After a career serving the Jewish community at JCCs and Jewish Family Service, Sam worked for the Elizabeth Public Schools and as an Instructor in Social Work for Rutgers University. He was raised in Malverne, NY and moved to New Jersey with his wife Maureen in 1999.  
  
Full_Season_Schedule_for_Midday_MeetUps: https://shomrei.org/calendar/midday-meetup-calendar  
  
**Questions?** Contact June Zimmerman,   
VP for Programming  
jzimmerman@shomrei.org  
  
\#middaymeetup

title: Everything Book Club, "Happiness" by Aminatta Forna
description:
A meditation in fiction on love, trauma, chance, and the nature of wild things--perfect for Earth Day. A Ghanaian psychiatrist and an American biologist who studies urban foxes bump into each other on Waterloo Bridge in London and their fates entwine when they get involved in a search for a missing boy. By a Scottish and Sierra Leonean writer whose books have been translated into 22 languages.  

“An exquisite novel about how chance and love connect us.”—The Washington Post

Aminatta Forna is a Scottish and Sierra Leonean writer whose books have been translated into 22 languages. She spent periods of her childhood in Iran, Thailand and Zambia. She is also the author of three other novels, including "The Memory of Love" as well as two works of non-fiction. She is a Fellow of the Royal Society of Literature

description:

title: 5K Training - No Boundaries - Orientation Night
location: Montclair - Anderson Park, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class.

Click here to register for the early bird price, available until April 3rd.

Click here for registrations April 3rd and later.

Have you always wanted to run or walk a 5K but didn't know how to begin training? Maybe you are nervous or don't want to do it alone? Maybe you have had a hard time sticking to a training program in the past? If you answered "Yes" to any of the above, our iconic beginner program is perfect for you. Often the first step is the hardest.

The camaraderie of a group and experience of your coaches and mentors provide great support toward healthier habits and lifestyle. This program is designed for people just starting a walking/running routine for the first time, restarting after a winter hiatus and those who want to run faster or longer. In 8 weeks, meeting twice a week, our fun, unintimidating program will help you walk, walk-run or run across the finish line at the Fitzgerald 5k Lager Run in June.

In collaboration with Dawn and John Fabbro of Fleet Feet Montclair

title: Awakening from Grief
description:
Awakening from Grief: Finding the Way back to Joy 
April 7th & 14th, 7:00 PM – 9:30 PM 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Van Vleck House is pleased to present AWAKENING FROM GRIEF: Finding the Way Back to Joy – one of our most popular workshops. Led by John E. Welshons, and based on his critically acclaimed book of the same title, AWAKENING FROM GRIEF offers the opportunity to explore – and heal – the psychological, emotional and spiritual challenges that follow in the wake of a loss or unexpected change. This workshop helps participants to discover the inner resources that will aid them in working through life’s inevitable challenges. We will gather in the living room of the Van Vleck Main House. Our time together will combine lecture, discussion, and a variety of meditative exercises. Participants complete the workshop feeling a profound sense of relief . . . and hope. Reading Awakening From Grief prior to the class is recommended, though not a requirement. 
Fee: Series (includes 2 classes) $65 for Members $80 for General Public 
Pre Registration is required by Monday, April 6th.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: 2026 Class Gift Tabling - Information and Philanthropy Cord Pick-Up
description:
Each year, our graduating class participates in a proud Class Gift tradition where members of the class invest in the University by supporting areas of Montclair State University that are most important to them. We invite the Class of 2026 to come together to show your support!
All gifts to Montclair count! Make your gift to any area of campus you feel most passionate about – such as the Emergency Book Fund, The Red Hawk Pantry, your school or college, The Fund for Montclair State or athletic program.
Your gift will pay it forward for future Red Hawks and kick-start your philanthropic connection to Montclair.

Commemorate your class year! Make a gift of at least $20.26 and you’ll receive your customized philanthropy cord to wear over your commencement robe. 
For a gift of $40, celebrate this milestone with your family, friends and colleagues by having your message displayed on the screen at the Commencement you will be participating in, and receive a customized philanthropy cord.

How do I get my customized philanthropy cord?
Customized philanthropy cords will be available for pick-up:

Tuesday, October 28 from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Thursday, November 20  from 1:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Tuesday, December 2 from 12:00 – 3:00 p.m. in the Student Center Lobby
Bloomfield Campus Graduation Fair: Wednesday, December 10th from 12:00 – 6:00 p.m. in Deacon’s Den, Talbott Hall (for Bloomfield graduates only)
Montclair Campus Graduation Fairs: Thursday, December 11th from 10:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m. in the Student Center Ballrooms (for Montclair or Bloomfield graduates)

Graduates can also arrange to pick up their philanthropy cords in the Overlook Corporate Center, 150 Clove Road, Little Falls, NJ during business hours, Monday through Friday, 8:30 a.m. – 4:30 p.m. Philanthropy cords can also be mailed to those who cannot pick it up. The last day we will mail the cords is Monday, December 22, 2025 at 2:30 p.m. Please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving at stewartj@montclair.edu or 973-655-5328 to finalize options to get your cords for the Winter Commencement.
Every student has benefitted from donor generosity to areas of academic support, emergency funding for students and overall priorities to boost students’ success every day. Thank you for paying it forward!For more information, please contact James Stewart, Associate Director of Annual Giving, at 973-655-5328 or stewartj@montclair.edu.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095723

title: Home Repair Essentials NEW
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register. 

Learn the fundamentals of home repair in this practical, hands-on workshop taught by Robert Haas, instructor of the popular Woodworking 101 and Advanced Woodworking classes. Held at Montclair High School, this course gives participants a behind-the-walls look at how common home features are built and maintained.

During the class, Robert will construct two demonstration walls that include doors and a window, showing how these elements are installed and how typical wall structures are assembled. Participants will also learn how traditional double-hung windows operate and how they can be repaired, including basic glass pane replacement.

The workshop will also introduce the basics of residential wiring, demonstrating how outlets and lighting are installed and connected. Throughout the session, Robert will answer questions and offer guidance on a wide range of home repair topics.

Whether you're a homeowner looking to handle basic repairs yourself or simply curious about how things in your home work, this class provides practical knowledge you can use right away.

title: Yom HahShoah Program: After the Holocaust: Family History, Memory, and Identity
description:
**Community Program:**   
7:15PM: Light Meal  
7:40PM - 9:00PM: Program  
  
**After the Holocaust:** **Family History, Memory, and Identity**  
In the aftermath of World War II, Jewish survivors in Displaced Persons (DP) camps rebuilt their lives with remarkable resilience, creating families and ushering in an unprecedented baby boom. At the same time, family memory—preserved through stories, documents, and traditions—became a powerful means of reconstructing identity after profound loss. In this lecture, Dr. Serafima Velkovich explores how family history research and personal narratives intersect with the history of DP camps and demonstrates how individuals can uncover traces of former DP babies within their own family histories.  
  
*Join a conversation with Dr. Sera Velkovich of Yad Vashem and Shirley Grill of Shomrei Emunah as they explore the resilience, and rebuilding of lives in the aftermath of the Shoah.*

title: The Twilight Zone: The Supernatural: Enchanted Objects - Mystical Forces NEW
location: Montclair Main - Conference Room, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

Nothing says The Twilight Zone like stories about enchanted objects, mystical forces or the presence of magic. Though they are interesting elements in the stories, the real intrigue lies with how the episodes’ characters behave in their presence. Driven by desperation, curiosity or just plain fear, we get the opportunity to witness lessons learned whether the result is salvation or human downfall.


During five episodes, we will see various responses to the unexplained. Whether they are controlled by superstition, are mishandling seemingly ordinary objects or dismissing what is not fully understood, each character must face the life-changing consequences
of their decisions.


Please join Linda Hetcher this season in the interactive study of the supernatural, most notably presented in The Twilight Zone.

title: JSON in the world of MSSQL
description:
Cloud Data Driven
JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a language in depended, very popular, open standard format for data exchange between applications and services.

JSON support was introduced in MS SQL 2016, enhanced in 2022. With the new "JSON data type", is now the time to finally complete the story and give to JSON everything he deserves?
On this session accompanied by numerous examples we should pass through the usage and support of JSON in MS SQL.

title: A Night of Jazz with the Bradford Hayes Quartet
description:
The Bradford Hayes Quartet featuring Bradford Hayes on Saxophone, Michael Cochrane on Piano, Takashi Otsuka on Bass and Joe Brown, Jr. on Drums will perform a set of originals and classic jazz hits. 

About the Artist
Bradford Hayes, a native of Dinwiddie County, Virginia has been a force on the jazz scene for many years now. He has performed at a number of clubs, colleges and concert halls that include Birdland (NYC), L'Entrepot (Paris, FRANCE) and the Beacon Theater (NYC). Bradford has performed in different musical settings with Jimmy Heath, Al Grey, Cecil Payne, Rufus Reid, Ray Bryant, Ted Curson, Charles Davis, Joe Lee Wilson, Ben Riley, Dr. Lonnie Smith, Cecil Brooks III, Steve Colson, Kelvin Bell, Pheeroan Aklaff, Michael Cochrane, James Weidman, Nabaté Isles and Donald Braden. Mr. Hayes’ band has also shared the stage with the bands of Gerald Albright, Chico Freeman, Betty Carter, Joe Henderson, Max Roach, Marlena Shaw, Pieces of a Dream, and Hilton Ruiz among others. In addition to leading his own band, Bradford worked with Babatunde Olatunji’s Drums of Passion Band for 15 years until Mr. Olatunji’s passing. Bradford, along with Javon Jackson, Hilton Ruiz, John Lee, and Bill Saxton, was photographed by legendary jazz photographer William Gottlieb in what sadly turned out to be the last jazz photo taken by Mr. Gottlieb. This was the first photo of musicians taken by Mr. Gottlieb since 1948. In addition to this photo session Bradford and the other musicians selected, are also featured as part of a soon to be aired PBS documentary about Mr. Gottlieb. Bradford recently performed a series of concerts in collaboration with the New Jersey Symphony Orchestra as well as a performance with Avant Garde Pianist Steve “Adegoke” Colson at the New Jersey Performing Arts Center.

Mr. Hayes holds a Bachelor of Sciences Degree in Music Education from North Carolina A&T State University in Greensboro, North Carolina. He also studied privately with George Coleman and Charles Davis.

www.bradfordhayes.com

title: Advanced Woodworking
location: Montclair - Montclair High School, Montclair Public Library
description:
This is a paid Adult School class. Click here to register.

For those with previous woodworking experience, this class will allow students to learn more advanced techniques. Students will have the opportunity to start a new project or bring in a project from home that they would like to work on.

This course will cover:
- Advanced use of saws, drills, routers, kreg jigs, dowel jigs, biscuit jointers, table saw, router table
- Various types of joinery, fasteners, and finishing
- Different edge finishes on router table
- Carving tools
- Corner clamps, bar clamps

PREREQUISITE: Completion of Woodworking 101

Materials fee is dependent on project and payable to instructor at the beginning of class.
Small Shelves / Spice Rack / Record Storage Box: $20-$25
Shelves / End Tables / Night Stands / Stools: $60-75
Coffee Table: $100

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
description:
Flute Masterclass with Jennifer Grim
Jennifer Grim, professor at the University of Miami, will present a masterclass for the Cali flute studio, where students will perform and receive individualized feedback.
Praised by The New York Times as “a deft, smooth flute soloist,” Jennifer Grim is a versatile performer whose work spans solo, chamber, and orchestral stages worldwide.
A member of the flute-percussion duo Solidaire and contemporary ensemble Flagelot, she has collaborated with leading groups including the Boston Chamber Music Society and Chamber Music Society of Lincoln Center. Her acclaimed album Through Broken Time reflects her strong commitment to contemporary music.
Grim is Professor and Chair of Instrumental Performance at the Frost School of Music, University of Miami, and serves as President of the National Flute Association Board.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12353914

title: Council On Ministries- Multi Platform
description:
The UUCM Council on Ministries (CoM) exists to report to UUCM, its Board and Co-Ministers on how our programs are accomplishing their particular mission while also furthering the broader mission of UUCM. Special attention is paid to any systemic or cultural obstacles hindering our ability to fulfill our mission as well as any conflicts. Its primary goals are to maintain UUCM's Mission & Vision, restore trust and relationship when frayed, and to keep all our interactions in line with UUCM's covenant. Best practices, connectivity, cross-pollination and successes are also reported. The CoM also provides assistance evaluating our ministries periodically at the discretion of the Board. Please welcome Council on Ministries liaisons into your meetings and planning when they request to observe your functioning; share with them also your minutes and other documents when requested. They are charged by the Board to help us have a broad view of our function as it relates to living out our mission and vision, so let us all assist them in this important 'meta-work.'

title: Eagle Rock Reservation
description:
Let's Walk!!
JOIN US FOR A WALK. WE WALK AT A MODERATE PACE ON UNPAVED PATHS.

We will hike for about an hour - 3.5 miles. We will be walking on trails in the woods, so appropriate footwear, long pants & socks, and bug spray.

Will will meet at:
[https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7](https://goo.gl/maps/7rRRTiXCntdE1neD7) Eagle Rock Trail Parking Lot by Kiosk#1

If you enter from Eagle Rock Way, go pass the Highlawn and stay left. There will be a parking lot at the end of the curve to your right.

Eagle Rock Reservation is 408 acres of hills, streams, and valleys located on the borders of West Orange, Verona, and Montclair, New Jersey. Though located in the middle of the most densely populated county in the most densely populated state in the country, the Reservation is mostly undeveloped and boasts an extensive trail system within its borders.

**Please make sure to RSVP and arrive 5 minutes prior to start time.**

FOLLOW US ON INSTAGRAM - letswalkmeetup

Disclaimer: If you join us for a hike, please understand that you are responsible for your own preparedness and well-being and will hold no one else liable in case of injury or mishap. Event will be canceled in the event of extreme weather.

title: CALI SCHOOL OF MUSIC | Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark
description:
Tuba Masterclass with Willie Clark
Willie Clark, professor of tuba at University of Maryland and former tuba with the United States AIr Force Band will lead Cali brass students in a masterclass focusing on the fundamentals of expressive brass playing across a landscape of genres.
Bio
Willie Clark is a former member of the United States Air Force Ceremonial Brass and a founding member of the professional Disney tuba quartet, The Tubafours. An internationally active performer, recording artist, and clinician, he has toured extensively throughout the United States, South America, Asia, Australia, and Europe, performing with leading orchestras, ensembles, and popular artists. Clark currently serves on the tuba faculty at the University of Maryland and Howard University, and is widely respected for his engaging teaching and expressive approach to brass performance.

Hosted by: The College of the Arts

title: April Coffee House Night RA+P
description:
Coffeehouse is our live music event where students can sign up to perform in Russ Lounge. Whether you’re playing, singing, or just coming to hang out, it’s always a great night of creativity and community.
The goal of the event is to turn Russ Lounge into a performance space that can accommodate an audience, but it’s also a hands-on opportunity to learn how to set up a performance from start to finish. We will be mixing on a Midas M32R, so it's also an opportunity to learn how digital mixers for live audio work. All that prep work culminates in a show featuring Montclair State students from 7 PM to 9:15 PM. We go through the process of setup, performance, and breakdown. IF this interests you, please fill out this form for more information on how to join the crew.
See you soon, stay groovy!!
All students are welcome, commuters need to be signed in a with a resident following Residence Life guest registration procedures.

title: Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes)
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekend Classes) 
Saturdays, 9:30 AM – 10:30 AM 
March 28th – Cancelled 
April 18th 
May 9th 
June 13th 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. Each class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Cold or rainy weather – class will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee per class:  $20 Members / $25 General Public 
Pre Registration is required.  There are no drop-ins for this class.

title: EARTH WEEK CELEBRATION 2026
description:
The Northeast Earth Coalition (NEEC) invites you to join the 2026 Earth Week Celebration April 18-26, for a week of events on the theme of “OUR POWER OUR PLANET.” Between Saturday April 18 and Sunday April 26, we will have volunteer opportunities for cleanups in parks, public spaces, and community gardens. 
The Northeast Earth Coalition is partnering with the Township of Montclair, the Parks and Recreation Committee, Friends of Montclair Parks, and The Brookdale Park Conservancy. 
 
  
THE NEEC IS PART OF THE GREAT GLOBAL CLEANUP® MOVEMENT!
MONTCLAIR EARTH DAY EVENTS: 
APRIL 18, 10-noon:  

Open House at Pine Street Community Garden – come check out the garden and learn about the new solar-powered Rocket composter

APRIL 22, 7-9 pm: 

Movie screening: “The Little Things That Run the World” – part of the Bee City USA series.
Guild Room, First Congregational Church, Montclair

APRIL 25, 10-11:30 am: 

Crane Park Pollinator Garden clean up – help us get the garden ready for Spring, and see what’s already growing! 

APRIL 26, 12-4 pm:

Montclair Earth Fest
South Park St. between Bloomfield Ave. and Church St.


 
For additional information & volunteer opportunities, please e-mail us at: Info@neearth.org or check our calendar for other events and workshops. 

Make every day Earth Day

Fight climate change with diet change
Plant native plants
Stand against deforestation
Organize community cleanups
Compost
Reduce plastic use
Go pesticide free
Turn off lights when not in use
Save paper – switch to online billing
Support organic agriculture

title: Underground Church Worship Service,10:30am on the Labyrinth in the Sanctuary
description: Underground Church (usually every 3rd Saturday) is a unique and dynamic gathering that meets monthly, and combines the intimacy of Jesus' early community with creative and inventive worship experiences.  In the warmth and safety of community we explore the scriptures, live out the questions through discussion,  and share innovative rituals that bring us closer to each other and to our still-speaking God. Together we create the unity of spirit that helps us find God's presence in the messy and beautiful experience of living today.  All are welcome.

title: Roller Skating Hang Out - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
Join us for an hour or two of roller skating indoors at the Florham Park Roller Rink, 192 Ridgedale Ave. No experience is needed. All you have to do is show up! Come anytime. Open skate is tentatively scheduled from 1 p.m. until 3 p.m.

The cost is $16 per person, unless you happen to have your own skates. Then, it's $10! You walk inside, pay at the window on your own, and then you'll see a growing group of people around one of the first sets of tables likely between 12:50 and 1:20 p.m. Some parking is available on site.

If looking to learn more, here's the website link for the roller rink -> www.myrink.com.

\*\*\* We will likely hang out after skating at the Florham Park Diner, 182 Ridgedale Ave. Feel free to post your ideas below in the comments section. Last time, we went to a place called Recess, a bar with pool tables, shuffleboard, a dart board and video games in Parsippany, thanks to a suggestion from Nigel. \*\*\*

Questions? Suggestions? Concerns? You can message me on Meetup or WhatsApp, or call/text me at 201-234-9609. Or feel free to comment below.

This event idea comes to you courtesy of Joe, one of our many regulars. Hope you'll consider attending our 7th skating event, or joining us for a bowling, volleyball, tennis or other event -> www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball

title: Forest Regeneration Volunteer Workday
description:
South Mountain Conservancy Activities Meetup Group
Forest Regeneration activities focus on the areas in and around the fenced 14-acre Forest and Wildflower Preserve located near the Dog Park on Bear Lane in the Reservation. On workdays, we focus on removing invasive plants like knotweed, garlic mustard and mugwort. Ages 12 to 15 are welcome with a parent or guardian present. Ages 15 and above require a signature by a parent or guardian on a [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi); teenagers cannot just be dropped off.

Please arrive 5 to 10 minutes early so we can hike into the Wildflower Preserve at 9 am sharp.

Please dress appropriately! There is some poison ivy on trails and in work areas. Wear long pants, long-sleeved shirt, closed-toe shoes or rain boots, bug spray, and layer as needed. We supply tools. If you have gloves, bring them or you can borrow a pair from us. Bring your own water.

You can download a copy of our [liability waiver](https://1drv.ms/b/c/d5fb9d3835660a78/IQCJO4IIj6GnQ6N4RuNC981xAYL2kFajs9_RL8f8V7NkhaM?e=5bd5oi) to print, sign and bring to the event.

We occasionally use photos of events in promotional materials and social media. By attending the event, I consent to photos of me being used in promotional materials or on social media.

For information, contact program co-coordinators Rich Rockwell and Tom Pollard at forestregen@[somocon.org](http://somocon.org/)

To see what's blooming in the Wildflower Preserve, check out our [Wildflower Preserve](https://www.inaturalist.org/projects/south-mountain-conservancy-wildflower-preserve) project on iNaturalist project.

**Meet at the tool sheds at the Dog Park at 12 Bear Lane, Maplewood NJ**
**GPS:** 40.739791, -74.289181

title: Shomrei Gives Back - Mitzvah Day
description:
**2nd Annual Mitzvah Day**  
  
Gather as a community in service to perform mitzvot for our local social action partners, Israel, and Shomrei itself. All ages welcome! There is a Mitzvah for everyone, including kids! Babysitting available for 1st grade and under. Breakfast nosh provided.  
  
Open to Shomrei Community & Your Friends.  
  
**Participate in any or all activities:**   
  
DONATE SUPPLIES** Donate items needed to make our Mitzvot a reality. Visit our online wish list to make a purchase. Make sure items are delivered to Shomrei **by April 8.** Drop-offs at Shomrei are welcome. Or, fund the supplies with a monetary donation. You don't have to donate items to participate (but it helps!)  
**online wish list: https://shomrei.org/wishlist**  
**alternative monetary donation:** **https://shomrei.org/supplies**  
 **VOLUNTEER YOUR TIME on April 19:**  
- Assemble Senior grocery bags for Toni's Kitchen
- Sort donated spring & summer clothes for Human Needs Food Pantry
- Assemble "Welcome to Your New Home" kits for Family Promise
- Make sandwiches for MESH
- Garden outdoors at Shomrei
- Make friendship bracelets for a school in Israel (kid-specific)
- Color pictures/write letters for seniors (kid-specific)
- Write thank you cards for pet adoptions for the Montclair Animal Shelter (kid-specific)

  
**register here: https://shomrei.org/mitzvah  
  
**PARTICIPATE IN A DRIVE**  
Bring breakfast cereals and/or adult and children’s summer clothing for Human Needs Food Pantry to Shomrei (Drop off at Shomrei or bring with you on Mitzvah day.)  
  
Key fob required for building entry.  
   
**Questions?**  
Contact the Office  
office@shomrei.org  
(973) 746-5031

How do I sign-up?

Registration opens on Fusion March 1st
Register online through Fusion, in the Student Recreation Center's main office, or over the phone (973-655-3340).
Cost for Students $10; Faculty/Staff $20

When and where?

Sunday, April 19, 2026
12:00 pm - 5:00 pm
Begins at Student Recreation Center

title: Interfaith Climate Action Committee Movie Night
Second Sunday Concert - Cart & Marrazza (rescheduled date)
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Rescheduled from December, 2025 due to snowstorm. The previous reservations for this performance will be honored. Unclaimed seats will be available right before the start of the concert. There is no waitlist. 
  
Now April 19, 2026 – Marrazza-Cart Duo 
HOPE WILL NEVER BE SILENT 
Robert Cart, flutist, and Regina DiMedio-Marrazza, piano, are a chamber ensemble, renowned for
passionate and musically stirring performances throughout the United States, including concerts at Carnegie Hall, the Kennedy Center, the Philadelphia Ethical Society, the Philadelphia International Music Festival, the National Flute Association, and several colleges and universities. The duo has premiered new works by Jennifer Higdon and Gary Schocker. Advocates of new music, they have also premiered new works by Louis Barroso, Jennifer Higdon, and Gary Schocker. They have graced our stage at Van Vleck House many times and are a series favorite with concert-goers. 
All Second Sunday concerts begin at 2PM. Seats are limited and reservations are recommended. If registered guests do not attend, those seats will be open to visitors right before the start of the concert.

title: Safe Space Training
description:
Our Safe Space Training is an intensive, interactive training that provides attendees with information and tools to combat homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, cissexism and heterosexism, on campus and beyond. Through interactive activities and discussion, participants will personally reflect on their own experiences to enhance their personal and professional development, provide further education, and finally, identify ways to create change on campus and beyond.
All sessions are three hours and are first-come, first-served. Pre-registration is required. Registrants are expected to attend the full session. Only individuals who have fully completed the Safe Space Program and have agreed to the expectations of its membership are able to display the sticker/button of our program. Refreshments are not provided; however, you are more than welcome to bring your lunch, coffee, snacks, etc.

Hosted by: Advocacy and Outreach

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/11332856

title: Garden to Table - Butterfly Waffles
description:
Garden to Table 
Mondays, 4:00 PM – 5:00 PM 
April 20 – Butterfly Waffles 
When exploring the gardens you are sure to spot monarch butterflies. Join us for our April Garden to Table class to make Butterfly Waffles, and learn more about why monarchs are so important to have in our gardens. 
Enjoy quality time in the garden with your child/children as we explore the Edible Garden and all that it has to offer. Help us weed and water, plant, and harvest. We will prepare a simple snack with ingredients inspired by the garden. Appropriate for children ages 4 and up but all are welcome. Children must be accompanied by an adult. 
Sponsored by  
Fee: $15/class per child for Members / $20/class per child for General Public 
Registration required by 1 day prior to each class

description:
On behalf of EngageNJ, we are excited to announce our 7th Annual THRIVE Conference will be hosted at Montclair State University, 7th Floor Conference Center on April 21st from 9:00AM - 3:00 PM!
 
Conference Highlights: 
This conference is FREE to all attendees! 
Gain insights from professionals and student leaders who are paving the way for the future generation.
Breakfast & lunch are provided. 
 
Click here to register  https://forms.monday.com/forms/adb42295b54232821c8d33cbc5ffee16?r=use1 
 
The deadline to register for this conference is Monday, April 6th.
 
We are looking for presenters! We are seeking workshops centered around professional development, student leadership, or community building. The workshop should be 45 minutes in total. The deadline to submit this form is March 31st.
 
If you have any questions, please feel free to reach out to perezso@montclair.edu or info@engagenj.org

title: upLIFT
description:
Step into the brand-new PLAE Room and discover upLIFT—a judgement-free program designed to help every student, from complete beginners to seasoned gym-goers, feel confident and capable with free-weight training. Learn proper lifting technique, get personalized form checks, and master the basics of creating your own effective workout programs. Whether you’re looking to meet other students who love to lift, pursue new fitness goals, or simply feel more at home in the weight room, upLIFT offers a supportive community and a dedicated space to grow. The program is structured for steady progression throughout the semester, but you’re welcome to drop in anytime and join at your own pace.

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: The DJ Academy: Music Matters 101
description:
DJing is one of the original elements of Hip Hop and is often considered the first. Hip Hop began in the Bronx during the 1970s at community block parties, where DJs used turntables to bring people together through music. Early pioneers like DJ Kool Herc introduced breakbeats by looping the most rhythmic parts of records, creating the foundation for breaking and shaping how dancers and MCs interacted with the music.
As Hip Hop evolved, DJing became the musical backbone of the culture. DJs provide the beats for MCing, set the tempo for dancers, and control the energy of Hip Hop spaces by reading the crowd and responding in real time. Through techniques like scratching, cutting, and beat juggling, DJs helped define Hip Hop’s sound while preserving its history and passing down cultural knowledge.
This class introduces the basics of DJing and how music shapes the dance experience. DJ Bascis will explain core concepts such as tempo, rhythm, and song structure, helping students understand how music guides movement and energy on the dance floor.
Participants will learn how DJs choose music, create smooth transitions, and adjust the vibe based on the room. 
Through simple examples and live demonstrations, students will build confidence in listening to music with intention. This can bewhether they are dancing, freestyling, or interested in DJing themselves.
This class is designed for beginners and college students, with no prior DJ or music experience required.

Hosted by: Campus Activities at Bloomfield

Additional Information can be found at: https://montclair.campuslabs.com/engage/event/12095724

Participants will also explore strategies for working with AI responsibly, emphasizing informed decision-making and human-centered AI practice. title: Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn
description:
Why Godzilla Still Matters: Montclair State Professor Jason Abbott to Speak in Brooklyn
Dr. Jason Abbott, Professor of Political Science and International Relations at Montclair State University, will deliver a public lecture as part of the Lectures on Tap series in Brooklyn, New York.
 
Title: Godzilla: Japan, Trauma, and the Unkillable MonsterDate: Wednesday, April 22, 2026Time: 6:30 p.m. – 8:30 p.m. (Lecture begins at 7:00 p.m.)Location: Bushwick, Brooklyn, NY (exact address provided upon registration)Please Register: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/lectures-on-tap-godzilla-japan-trauma-and-the-unkillable-monster-tickets-1985135400421?aff=oddtdtcreator
 
Lectures on Tap is a public lecture series that brings professors, experts, and storytellers into informal venues to deliver accessible, thought-provoking talks.
 
In this lecture, Dr. Abbott examines Godzilla as a cultural lens through which to understand Japan’s postwar experience. Emerging in the shadow of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, Godzilla has long served as a cinematic reflection of national trauma, nuclear anxiety, and unresolved historical memory. The talk explores why the figure of Godzilla reappears at moments of crisis and what this enduring monster reveals about how societies process catastrophe and construct collective memory.
 
Drawing on examples across multiple films and historical periods, the lecture considers how trauma is not simply resolved, but often reworked and reimagined over time.
Agenda:6:30 p.m. – Doors open; seating, food, and drinks available6:55 p.m. – Host introduction7:00 p.m. – Lecture begins7:45 p.m. – Audience Q&A8:00 p.m. – Informal discussion and networking8:30 p.m. – Event concludes

Hosted by: College of Humanities and Social Sciences (CHSS)

title: Movie screening: "The Little Things That Run the World" - Earth Week Celebration
location: Guild Room at First Congregational Church, 40 South Fullerton Avenue, Montclair, New Jersey, 07042, 07042, United States
description:
Join us for a screening of “The Little Things That Run the World,” part of our Bee City USA program. 
Synopsis:
Life on Earth is in the middle of a major re-alignment of species composition. The smallest of creatures – insects – hold the fabric of all life together. They are the most numerous of all animal groups on the planet. But insects are declining at an alarming rate throughout the world, threatening innumerable species, whole ecosystems, and even our food systems with collapse. This is story about untangling the mysteries as to why something so ubiquitous – and seemingly endless – could be on the cusp of disappearing entirely. It is also the story of heroic efforts to save these most vulnerable of creatures. 
Admission is free. Register to save your seat. 
Learn more about the film here.

title: Agentic AI Finance: Governed Metrics, Open Access, Automated Action on Databrick
description:
Cloud Data Driven
Large finance organizations already rely on dashboards and reports, but the real challenge is enabling automation without breaking governance or auditability. This session shares how a global finance organization implemented agent-driven workflows on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform using Unity Catalog, metrics views, and open lakehouse patterns.

The talk walks through a real implementation where finance metrics are defined once using metrics views, protected with attribute-based access control and automated classification, and reused consistently across reporting, analytics, and automation. Power BI connects directly to governed lakehouse tables and metrics for enterprise reporting, while Iceberg-compliant clients consume Delta tables through Uniform, enabling open access without duplicating data.

Agentic workflows observe certified metrics and events, assist with variance investigation, anomaly triage, and operational follow-ups, and propose actions that remain traceable and reviewable. Early results indicated several hours of manual investigation effort saved per reporting cycle and faster anomaly triage, without introducing additional governance or audit risk. The session includes an architecture walkthrough and demo using synthetic data, reflecting real finance constraints, trade-offs, and lessons learned when moving from reporting to governed, open, and action-oriented analytics.

"And they devoted themselves to the apostles' teaching and the fellowship, to the breaking of bread and the prayers. And awe came upon every soul, and many wonders and signs were being done through the apostles. And all who believed were together and had all things in common. And they were selling their possessions and belongings and distributing the proceeds to all, as any had need. And day by day, attending the temple together and breaking bread in their homes, they received their food with glad and generous hearts, praising God and having favor with all the people. And the Lord added to their number day by day those who were being saved." - Acts 2:42-47

description:

title: Casual Volleyball - North Jersey
description:
North Jersey Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis And More (BVT)
We're a group of people who will start by hitting the ball around for the first 20-30 minutes and then we'll break up into 2-4 teams for two or three hours of play.

If we have more than 20 people show up, we'll break up into three or four teams of 6 (Some may have 7, 8 or 9. Additional players just rotate into play). Games will be to 10 points. Rally scoring. Winner stays on (unless a team has won four straight).

We'll be heading outdoors to the hard court in Leonia off Overpeck County Pk Driveway in the Overpeck County Park.

\*\*\* After playing, we'll be likely heading to Tropical Juice Bar, an idea of regular attendee Kate, for a drink and snack. It's located at 60 River St. in Hackensack. Feel free to join us or suggest another spot for a post-volleyball hangout. \*\*\*

There's no fee to play volleyball and nothing else you need to do besides show up. Come anytime!! All are welcome!!! We're not in a league and are not here to analyze your form. We're more for people who haven't played in months, maybe years, and just want to occasionally play again and hang out.

Directions: You can Google "Volleyball Court in Leonia" and it should come up. Or type 41 Willow Tree Road, Leonia, NJ 07605 into your GPS, but note that it's not on Willow Tree Road, just near it. Instead, turn onto Overpeck County Pk Driveway off Fort Lee Road (Degraw Avenue). It is past the tennis courts and near a basketball court and track. You can try Googling Leonia High School as well. That should help you find it.

We don't have the court reserved and if it happens to be taken, we may play with those already occupying it or we will try to relocate on the fly. It's certainly possible that we may have to cancel that day if we can't find a suitable spot. Please keep that in mind. And if it's looking like it'll rain or snow, I'll make a call on whether to cancel around an hour ahead of time.

Please bring a ball and net if you have them for possible use during the game. Feel free to bring a speaker for music, or snacks and drinks for consumption during play.

If you have trouble finding the court or group, you can call or text Andy Milone at 201-234-9609. There is plenty of parking and an open bathroom at the park.

Hope you'll join us for our 18th volleyball event. Questions or comments? You can message Andy on Meetup, or call/text him at 201-234-9609.

Check out bowling, soccer, volleyball, tennis or one of our other activities at: www.meetup.com/north-jersey-bowling-soccer-volleyball
source: Meetup: NJ Bowling, Volleyball, Tennis & More

title: Jewish without Apology: A Conversation with Sarah Hurwitz
description:
**Seth Goodchild Memorial Lecture**  
Reception immediately following  
  
RSVP: http://shomrei.org/hurwitz  
  
**About the Program**  
Award-winning author Sarah Hurwitz joins Rabbi Julie Roth for a candid conversation about rediscovering Judaism as an adult and why its intellectual depth and moral clarity feel strikingly relevant to modern life. From finding meaning in a chaotic world to standing tall against rising antisemitism, Sarah explores why embracing Jewish wisdom isn’t just about honoring the past -- it’s about sanctifying the present and elevating our shared humanity.   
  
**Free Babysitting Available**  
Email Aviva Arad, aarad@shomrei.org  
  
**\*Additional parking is available next door at 73 & 77 Park Street.**  
  
**In Loving Memory of Seth Goodchild**   
Seth Goodchild was inspired by his family to convert to Judaism at the end of his life. To honor his legacy and share his love of Judaism, his friends and family sponsored this lecture and a class on exploring Judaism.

title: Meeting at the Verona inn!
description:
Board Game Night At Verona Inn
We will be doing our first monthly meeting starting on **February 25th at the Verona Inn!** We will be meeting up to organize games starting at 7:30, with food and drinks available courtesy of our host! You can play any game provided by hosts or bring your own! If you do not have a friend group to play games, hosts will organize you into pods with other people who need players. There are **no dues**. Future meetings will be held on the **last Wednesday of each month.**

More information and ground rules:

* **The doors open at 6:30 but games do not start until 7:30.** I *highly recommend* coming in during this period to eat your food and drink ahead of time!
*
* **Please return the graciousness provided by our hosts and order at least one item! All venue specific rules apply to our club.** Future meetings depend on treating our hosts with respect.
* **All games must be returned to their owners when they announce that they are leaving.** If you are in a game in progress when the meeting ends but it is not borrowed you may continue playing!
* **Please place food and drink on a separate table or surface from your games** to help avoid stains and other damage.
source: Meetup: Board Game Night at Verona Inn

title: Mindfulness in the Garden - Early Spring Series
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Mindfulness in the Garden (Weekday Series) 
Instructor: Jodi Brooks 
Tuesdays, 11:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Early Spring Session: Apr 7, 14, & 28 (No class 4/21) 
Discover how a mindfulness practice can help us awaken and  live in harmony with oneself and with the natural world. This class will include: simple instructions for getting started with a formal mindfulness practice, very gentle stretching & breathwork, and an outdoor contemplative garden walk. This is a practice of slowing down, cultivating quiet and paying attention to our senses. All levels are welcome. No experience needed. Winter session will be held indoors, with a short walk outdoors (weather permitting). 
Fee: 3 April classes: General public $75, Members $60 
Series registration only. Pre Registration is required – there are no drop-ins for this class. 
 
Late Spring Session: May 5, 12 19 & 26 
Early Summer Session: June 2, 9, 16 & 23

title: Weekly Labyrinth Walks, Wednesdays 11am-1pm
description:
Weekly: Wednesdays 11am-1pm in the Sanctuary

Take your soul for a walk on the labyrinth.  We are pleased to offer labyrinth walks each Wednesday in the mid-day as a chance to connect with your soul in our beautiful sanctuary. 

The labyrinth is an ancient spiritual tool , a mode of “walking prayer” that invites us to go deeper in our walk with God.  Our labyrinth, modeled on one in Chartes Cathedral in France, sits as the center of our Sanctuary, a constant metaphor for our spiritual lives.  Just like the labyrinth, our lives are not linear, but filled with twists and turns.  Sometimes we make great progress, and other times we feel we are going backwards.  But if we trust in the path we will find ourselves in the center -- the heart of God.

All are welcome to take their souls out for a healing walk... no experience necessary!  Check out this lovely write up on Baristanet from the Spring 2014.

Watch this video to find out more about walking the labyrinth. If you’ve never done so come out one Wednesday evening and give it a try. It is a wonderful way to nourish your soul and spend some quiet time in prayer or meditation.

title: Virtual Meditation
location: Van Vleck House & Gardens, 21 Van Vleck Street, Montclair, 07042, United States
description:
Virtual Meditation 
Instructor: John Welshons 
Wednesdays, 7:00 PM – 9:00 PM 
January 7, 14, 21, 28 
February 4, 11, 25 
March 4, 18, 25 
April 1, 8, 15, 22, 29 
May 6, 13, 20 
June 3, 10, 24 
Join contemporary spiritual teacher and author Ramananda John E. Welshons (One Soul, One Love, One Heart, When Prayers Aren’t Answered and Awakening From Grief) for a series of evening meditation classes in the comfort of your own home via Zoom. These classes will focus on specific practices for working with fear and anxiety.  Participants will learn and practice different forms of mindfulness meditation in each session and will have the opportunity to participate in discussions of Ramananda’s three critically acclaimed books. Open to beginners and experienced practitioners, these gatherings offer an opportunity to experience deep inner peace while practicing with a highly experienced meditation teacher.  
Important Updated Registration Information: 
A Zoom Link will be sent the day of the class. If you are registering after 3 PM on the day of the class, the Zoom link will be included in the automated payment confirmation email sent when you register. There is no registration deadline for this class. 
Fee: Single class: $10 Members / $15 General Public
